system.description: An incorporeal creature has no physical body. It can be harmed only by incorporeal creatures, magic weapons or creatures that strike as magic weapons, and spells, spell-like abilities, or supernatural abilities. It is immune to nonmagical attacks. Even from magic, it has a 50% chance to ignore damage from a corporeal source (except positive energy, negative energy, force, or ghost touch attacks). It has no natural armor but gains a deflection bonus to AC equal to its Charisma bonus (minimum +1). It has no Strength score; Dexterity applies to melee and ranged attacks. It moves silently and can pass through solid objects (with limitations; see SRD).
